evidence.snippet: The UEL range of lightweight gasoline – fuelled Wankel-type rotary engines covers 20-120bhp (15-90kw).
Engines for various size tactical UAV’s, target drones and single mission platforms.

items.notes: Wankel-type rotary gasoline engines (20–120 BHP) specifically designed for tactical UAVs, target drones, and single-mission platforms.

evidence.snippet: This policy pertains to this website by **UAV Engines Limited**, a company headquartered in Staffordshire, UK. This includes www.uavenginesltd.co.uk additional UAV Engines websites, and digital assets produced and or managed websites (together, the “Site”).

ENGINES FOR VARIOUS SIZE TACTICAL UAVS, TARGET DRONES AND SINGLE MISSION PLATFORMS
The UEL range of lightweight gasoline – fuelled Wankel-type rotary engines covers 20-120bhp (15-90kw).

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Manufactures and sells Wankel rotary engines that are integrated into UAV platforms by airframe OEMs; also offers engine integration application services.

items.name: AR731 – 38 BHP
items.product_type: Wankel rotary UAV target engine, air cooled, 38 BHP

evidence.snippet:
**AR731 – 38 BHP**
---------------------
UAV TARGET ENGINE
The AR731 has the highest power-to-weight ratio of any rotary engine in the world. It has been specifically designed and developed to be the ultimate engine for small target drones and short-life UAV’s. TBO is 10 to 50 hours depending on duty cycle.

evidence.snippet: We are approved by Lloyds Register to the highest level of certification appropriate to our position in the UK Aerospace manufacturing sector.
Leading power solutions for small & medium sized UAVs in over 25 countries worldwide.

readiness.notes: AS9100D / ISO 9001 certified; selling engines in over 25 countries for 30 years; published price list available on request.

tagline: Manufactures lightweight Wankel rotary engines (38–95 BHP) for tactical UAVs, target drones, and surveillance platforms; six production SKUs with FAR-33 endurance certification on select models. UK-based, ISO 9001/AS9100 accredited, 30 years operational. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown; minimal public evidence of US prime integration or current-generation platform adoption.
